title: betula papyrifera var. commutata
intro: Betula papyrifera var. commutata, commonly known as the white birch, is a deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its white, papery bark. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of white birch are numerous, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate cold and flu symptoms, and even help with skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of white birch can be found in the form of tea, salves, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe and heal irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
